title: Perpetuity: You are going to pay 800 into an account at...
summary: Calculate the effective annual rate or convert between APR and EAR under different compounding assumptions.
prompt: You are going to pay $800 into an account at the beginning of each of 20 years (First payment at t=0). The account will then be left to compound for an additional 20 years. At the end of the 41st year you will begin receiving a perpetuity from the account. If the account pays 14%, how much each year will you receive from the perpetuity (round to nearest $1,000)?
solution: To determine how much you will receive each year from the perpetuity, follow these steps: 1. **Calculate the Future Value of Annuity (FVA):** - You will pay $800 at the beginning of each of the first 20 years. This is an annuity due. - The formula for the future value of an annuity due is: \[ FVA = PMT \times \left(\frac{(1 + r)^n - 1}{r}\right) \times (1 + r) \] where: - \( PMT = 800 \) - \( r = 0.14 \) (annual interest rate) - \( n = 20 \) (number of years) 2. **Calculate the Amount after 20 Years:** \[ FVA = 800 \times \left(\frac{(1 + 0.14)^{20} - 1}{0.14}\right) \times (1 + 0.14) \] \[ FVA = 800 \times \left(\frac{(1.14)^{20} - 1}{0.14}\right) \times 1.14 \] \[ FVA = 800 \times \left(\frac{13.7432 - 1}{0.14}\right) \times 1.14 \] \[ FVA = 800 \times (91.0229) \times 1.14 \] \[ FVA = 800 \times 103.7651 \] \[ FVA = 83,012.08 \] 3. **Calculate the Amount after an Additional 20 Years:** - This amount will compound for another 20 years without additional payments. - The formula for future value of a lump sum is: \[ FV = PV \times (1 + r)^n \] where: - \( PV = 83,012.08 \) - \( r = 0.14 \) - \( n = 20 \) \[ FV = 83,012.08 \times (1 + 0.14)^{20} \] \[ FV = 83,012.08 \times (13.7432) \] \[ FV = 1,140,575.85 \] 4. **Calculate the Perpetuity Payment:** - The formula for the annual payment of a perpetuity is: \[ PMT = PV \times r \] where: - \( PV = 1,140,575.85 \) - \( r = 0.14 \) \[ PMT = 1,140,575.85 \times 0.14 \] \[ PMT = 159,680.62 \] Rounding to the nearest $1,000, the annual amount you will receive from the perpetuity is: \[ \boxed{160,000} \]
